topic: completeness of the SS-ID algorithm at its open cs_fail_2 failure line for data fusion under systematic selection (Lee-Ghassami-Shpitser, arXiv:2404.06602, UAI 2024): define the context-specific hedge (CS-hedge) as a graph-only correlation-hiding structure over label-set ADMG multigraphs, and prove the context-marginal sufficiency lemma — every admissible context U-severs one side, so each context law depends only on the uncoupled per-side marginals while the target Q = p(Y(a), S=empty), living in the un-severed S=empty world, depends on the coupled sum kappa — hence SS-ID is sound and complete, settling the authors' published conjecture

open_problems.open_problem: Does every SS-ID cs_fail_2 failure admit a graph-only context-specific hedge over the label-set ADMG multigraph that yields two compatible CS-SCMs agreeing on every admissible context law but disagreeing on the target interventional law?

source_refs.claim: The authors prove SS-ID sound and show nonidentification for all but one failure case, then explicitly conjecture completeness.
source_refs.author: Jaron J. R. Lee, AmirEmad Ghassami, Ilya Shpitser

source_refs.claim: For arbitrary surrogate experiments, hedgelets and thickets give a necessary-and-sufficient graphical condition and a complete gID algorithm.
source_refs.author: Sanghack Lee, Juan D. Correa, Elias Bareinboim

source_refs.excerpt: State explicitly which SS-ID failure line or branch the overlap-hedge conjecture targets
Conjecture 1 is the novel kernel and is not yet proved.

open_problems.why_unsolved: Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser adapt ordinary hedges or gID thickets to the SS-ID branches where a context supplies a conventional unreachable district, but leave cs_fail_2 without a converse. The banked predecessor independently proposed an overlap-hedge converse yet left it unproved, and its reviewers required an exact mapping to this failure line. The obstacle is simultaneous preservation of every selected context law and factor-local equality: ordinary correlation-hiding witnesses can leak their coupling parameter after label-induced context restriction, even though that parameter must remain visible in the unselected target world.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient theorem defining a graph-only CS-hedge and constructively proving that cs_fail_2 supports two admissible-context-law-equivalent CS-SCMs with different Q=p(Y(a),S=empty), thereby completing SS-ID.

writing_exemplar.why: It supplies the systematic-selection setup, CS-SCM semantics, SS-ID recursion, handled failure cases, and the explicit conjecture that the new result settles.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: auxiliary experiments can overcome ordinary confounding -> assignment into those datasets may itself be systematic and confounded -> SS-ID exploits selector-induced context restrictions -> cs_fail_2 alone lacks a graph-only nonidentification certificate

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt the binary parity/XOR paired-SCM hedge construction and its proof that observed laws agree while the target interventional law changes.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A CS-hedge is a context-labeled strengthening of the classical hedge; the new proof burden is equality after every label-induced context deletion.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt hedgelet/thicket assembly, whose proof synchronizes local nonidentification witnesses across a family of supplied experimental distributions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: SS-ID likewise requires one target-changing witness to preserve several input laws, augmented here by label-driven context restrictions.

method_exemplars.technique: For targets on the identified side of the characterization, derive source-wise pathwise derivatives, project onto the fused-data tangent space, and prove a cross-fitted one-step estimator is efficient.
method_exemplars.why_closest: An SS-ID output is a functional assembled from aligned context laws, matching the multi-source functional and inference structure treated there.

open_problems.open_problem: Under a candidate CS-hedge, do all admissible nonempty contexts sever at least one side strongly enough that each observed context law depends only on uncoupled per-side marginals, while the empty-context target retains dependence on their latent coupling?

source_refs.excerpt: boundary variables observed in several sources are pasted through common latent response coordinates
Add an explicit mapping from each typed source-cover input to the corresponding systematic-selection input

open_problems.why_unsolved: The predecessor proposed pasting shared boundary variables through common latent response coordinates, but did not prove that every admissible selection context removes the cross-side coupling from its observed law. The missing invariant is context-uniform: for every allowed label pattern, at least one side must be U-severed before marginalization. Without it, the paired construction can leak the coupling parameter into one available context and cease to be a valid nonidentification witness.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A context-marginal sufficiency lemma giving graph-theoretic U-severing conditions under which every admissible context law factors through uncoupled side marginals while Q=p(Y(a),S=empty) is a nonconstant function of their coupling.

writing_exemplar.why: Its context-indexed data laws and label-set graphs make equality in every revealed context the natural missing witness obligation.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: systematic selection reveals different context laws -> a countermodel pair must preserve every revealed law -> context-specific severing may erase cross-side coupling -> prove that all context laws see only the two marginals while the target sees their coupling

method_exemplars.technique: Use context-restricted latent projections and district recursion to verify, context by context, which side of the candidate witness is severed and which kernel factors remain reachable.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired lemma is a local invariant of the same label-set multigraph and fixing operations on which SS-ID runs.

method_exemplars.technique: Lift the ordinary parity/correlation-hiding hedge SCM, then prove its observable invariance separately after each context-specific graph deletion and marginalization.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The required coupled-versus-marginal separation is the context-indexed analogue of the classical hedge witness.

open_problems.open_problem: Can the three-source response-function null-direction template be promoted to a positivity-compatible generic open-set CS-hedge obstruction rather than a single boundary example?

source_refs.excerpt: Turn the three-source boundary template into a generic open-set obstruction condition
check whether the failed district leaves one free direction h

open_problems.why_unsolved: The predecessor specified a response-type linear system whose kernel might contain a target-changing direction, but did not prove that such a direction survives all context and invariance equations or that the base response-type law stays strictly positive under both signed perturbations. A degenerate boundary example cannot establish the robust, graph-level obstruction required by a completeness theorem.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sufficient-condition theorem producing a nonempty open set of positive context laws for which the CS-hedge constraint matrix has a target-changing null direction and both signed perturbations are compatible CS-SCMs.

writing_exemplar.why: Its unresolved completeness branch explains why a robust witness class, rather than an isolated numerical counterexample, is necessary.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: cs_fail_2 lacks a nonidentification certificate -> a finite response-function system exposes possible hidden directions -> a boundary null direction may be degenerate -> prove a positive open-set obstruction

method_exemplars.technique: Express the classical paired-SCM hedge perturbation as a constraint-null-space argument and prove sufficiently small signed perturbations preserve positivity and every supplied-law equality.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The requested object is the linear-algebraic form of a robust hedge witness: a target-changing direction invisible to all available laws.

method_exemplars.technique: Use thicket/hedgelet composition to assemble local nonidentification perturbations while preserving a collection of supplied distributions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its witness machinery is the closest existing method for maintaining several input distributions simultaneously.

open_problems.open_problem: Is a binary finite CS-SCM witness always sufficient for cs_fail_2 nonidentification under every label-set overlap constraint, or do some CS-hedges require larger observed or latent alphabets?

source_refs.excerpt: multisource overlap constraints may require a larger finite alphabet
Sharpen the binary-witness assumption ... or state an alphabet-preserving finite witness version.

open_problems.why_unsolved: Ordinary ID completeness admits finite binary witnesses, but the prior proposal warns that matching several selected overlaps and typed invariance constraints can overdetermine the binary response-function table. It did not establish whether the classical binary construction has enough remaining degrees of freedom or whether alphabet enlargement is essential for some label-set multigraphs.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Either an alphabet-preserving theorem constructing a binary CS-hedge witness for every cs_fail_2 graph, or a minimal label-set graph proving binary impossibility and identifying the smallest sufficient alphabet.

writing_exemplar.why: It fixes the graph and context semantics that any finite countermodel pair must realize.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: SS-ID failure needs an observationally equivalent model pair -> classical ID uses binary witnesses -> multiple context equalities may exhaust binary degrees of freedom -> determine binary sufficiency or the minimal required alphabet

method_exemplars.technique: Audit the ordinary binary response-function construction and track which parity degrees of freedom remain after imposing every context-law equality.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the exact binary witness whose robustness to label-set constraints is unresolved.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt thicket and hedgelet witness assembly for collections of input distributions, with an explicit alphabet audit across component witnesses.
method_exemplars.why_closest: General identifiability already imposes simultaneous equality of several supplied causal distributions, paralleling the multiple context-law constraints here.

literature_map:
Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser (UAI 2024, arXiv:2404.06602) introduce CS-SCMs and SS-ID for arbitrary confounding plus systematic selection among experimental regimes.
Their main theorem proves soundness; the paper states that all but one failure case imply nonidentification and explicitly conjectures full completeness.
The unresolved confounded-selector branch is tracked here as cs_fail_2; the paper does not provide a CS-hedge certificate for it.
Shpitser and Pearl (AAAI 2006, arXiv:1206.6876) provide the ordinary hedge and parity-countermodel template for complete ID.
Huang and Valtorta (UAI 2006, arXiv:1206.6831) independently establish completeness of do-calculus for ordinary causal-effect identification.
Lee, Correa, and Bareinboim (UAI 2019/PMLR 2020) extend nonidentification witnesses to arbitrary surrogate experiments through hedgelets and thickets.
Kivva, Mokhtarian, Etesami, and Kiyavash (UAI 2022, arXiv:2206.01081) show that gID soundness and completeness are positivity-sensitive and give a repaired positive-law algorithm.
Bareinboim and Pearl (JCI 2013, arXiv:1312.7485) give a complete transportability algorithm and s-hedge-style countermodels for cross-population shifts.
Bareinboim and Tian (AAAI 2015, doi:10.1609/aaai.v29i1.9679) study simultaneous selection and confounding, but not systematic context selection across experiments.
Correa, Tian, and Bareinboim (AAAI 2019) complete recovery from selection-biased data, not the context-selected multi-experiment problem.
Tikka, Hyttinen, and Karvanen (JSS 2021, arXiv:1902.01073) give sound do-search for arbitrary inputs while limiting completeness to characterized classes.
Nested-Markov reachability and fixing supply the kernel operations used by ID, gID, and SS-ID.
Li and Luedtke (Biometrika 2023, arXiv:2111.14945) provide source-aligned efficiency bounds and cross-fitted estimators after identification.
The live certificate-level tension is that ordinary hedges, thickets, and s-hedges need not remain correlation-hiding under every label-induced context marginal.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined the downgraded eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1 proposal, README, prior gaps ledger, and all eight substantive angle-review JSONs.
That predecessor is the direct catalogue match: its unproved Conjecture 1 targets the sole SS-ID failure branch outside the published hedge/thicket proof.
Reviewer angle0_v5 strengthening_paths[0] and angle1_v1 strengthening_paths demand an exact SS-ID branch mapping and systematic-selection encoding.
Reviewer angle2_v0 strengthening_paths[1] demands a generic open-set obstruction rather than the proposal's three-source boundary template.
Reviewer angle1_v0 strengthening_paths[3] demands a binary restriction or an alphabet-preserving finite witness theorem.
Proposal §11 explicitly leaves binary-witness sufficiency uncertain because multiple overlap constraints may require larger alphabets.
The proof route identifies latent-coordinate pasting and preservation of all selected source margins as the central missing argument.
Also inspected eid_fragmented_transport_support_v1; its support-cover question does not duplicate cs_fail_2.
Adjacent proxy, mediation, and SCM bank entries concern bridge failure, response types, or partial identification rather than systematic-selection completeness.
